turns.question: Can I get your phone number?
turns.answer: Sure, it's five five five, oh one two three.

turns.question: What's the best number to reach you at?
turns.answer: You can reach me at five five five, four five six seven.

turns.question: Could you read me your number one more time?
turns.answer: Of course, it's five five five, seven eight nine oh.

turns.question: Is this still the right number for you?
turns.answer: Yes, five five five, one two three four is still correct.

turns.question: Do you have a work number as well?
turns.answer: Yeah, my office line is five five five, three three three three.

turns.question: What's your area code again?
turns.answer: It's two one two, based out of New York.

turns.question: Can you give me your number, digit by digit?
turns.answer: Sure -- five, five, five, zero, one, two, three.

turns.question: Is your number still the one ending in double zero?
turns.answer: Yeah, it's five five five, six two double zero.

turns.question: What number should I use to text you?
turns.answer: Just use this one, five five five, nine nine one one.

turns.question: Do you mind giving me an emergency contact number?
turns.answer: Sure, it's my sister's number, five five five, two two four four.

turns.question: Could you double-check that number for me?
turns.answer: Sure, let me repeat it -- five five five, eight one two one.

turns.question: What's the customer service number for this company?
turns.answer: It's one eight hundred, five five five, one two three four.

turns.question: Is there a direct line I can call instead?
turns.answer: Yes, call five five five, seven seven zero zero directly.

turns.question: Can I text you at this same number?
turns.answer: Yep, same number works for texting too.

turns.question: What's the extension I need after the main number?
turns.answer: Dial the main number, then extension four five six.

turns.question: Do you know your new number by heart yet?
turns.answer: Almost, it's five five five, three one two zero.

turns.question: Is this number connected to WhatsApp too?
turns.answer: Yes, same number works on WhatsApp as well.

turns.question: What was that last part of the number again?
turns.answer: The last part was six seven eight nine.

turns.question: Can I reach the front desk at this number?
turns.answer: Yes, the front desk is five five five, four four zero zero.

turns.question: Is there a toll-free number I should use instead?
turns.answer: Yes, it's one eight hundred, five five five, zero one one one.

turns.question: What number did you call me from earlier?
turns.answer: I called from five five five, two two eight eight.

turns.question: Do you still use that old number from before?
turns.answer: No, I switched, it's five five five, nine zero zero one now.

turns.question: Can you confirm the number ends in seven three?
turns.answer: Yes, that's correct, ends in seven three.

turns.question: What's the fax number, if you still use one?
turns.answer: It's five five five, six six one two, believe it or not.

turns.question: Is that a mobile number or a landline?
turns.answer: It's my mobile, five five five, three four five six.

turns.question: Can I save this as your main contact number?
turns.answer: Yes, please do, five five five, one one two two.

turns.question: What number should the delivery driver call?
turns.answer: Have them call five five five, seven six five four.

turns.question: Is there a different number for after hours?
turns.answer: Yes, after hours, call five five five, zero zero nine nine.

turns.question: Can you spell out the country code for me?
turns.answer: Sure, it's plus one for the United States.

turns.question: What number is listed for the appointment reminder?
turns.answer: It shows five five five, eight eight zero zero.

turns.question: Is this the number I should use for the survey?
turns.answer: Yes, that's the correct number for the survey.

turns.question: Can you repeat the number slower this time?
turns.answer: Sure -- five, five, five... four, five, six... seven.

turns.question: What's the number for roadside assistance?
turns.answer: It's one eight hundred, five five five, four two one two.

turns.question: Is there a shorter number I can dial locally?
turns.answer: Yes, locally you can just dial five five five, two one one one.

turns.question: Do you know the number off the top of your head?
turns.answer: I do, it's five five five, one two nine eight.

turns.question: What number should I list as a reference?
turns.answer: You can list five five five, six five four three.

turns.question: Is the number the same across all their branches?
turns.answer: No, each branch has its own number.

turns.question: Can I get the number for the branch downtown?
turns.answer: Sure, it's five five five, two four six eight.

turns.question: What number appears on your caller ID?
turns.answer: It shows five five five, nine one nine one.

turns.question: Is this a good number to leave a voicemail?
turns.answer: Yes, this number has voicemail set up.
